manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: bond, tie, moon, string, czech, row, casino, concert, link.
Your opponent's team words are: bark, beat, platypus, pyramid, hotel, alien, robin, centaur.
Distractor words are: pass, belt, leprechaun, star, eye, tag, mug.
The kill words are: ninja.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of the team's words. Must avoid being any word from any list (team, opponent, distractors, kill). Also cannot contain parts of them. Provide "CLUE: <WORD>" and "TARGETS: <TARGETS>" comma-separated list of which of our team's words we intend the clue to point to.

We have team words: bond, tie, moon, string, czech, row, casino, concert, link.

We need to choose a clue that can hint at multiple of them. For instance "music" could relate to concert, string (instrument), tie? Not really. "Guitar" relates to string, concert maybe. But avoid being any word in list: "guitar" not present. Could clue "silver" relates to moon, tie (silver tie), maybe string (silver thread). Not great.

Maybe "connection" relates to bond, link, tie. That's three. Is "connection" acceptable? Not in any list. However might also hint at link/d... yes.

Could we also include "row" and "casino"? Maybe "Vegas" but that's a place, could hint at casino, maybe tie? Not.

Better to maximize safe picks: choose clue "bond" related words already. But clue cannot be a word in the list. "Bond" is a team word, so can't use it.

Potential clue: "relationship". Relates to bond, tie, link. Good. Also maybe string (relationship? no). Not in any list.

Check rule: clue cannot contain parts of them. "relationship" contains "ship" but not a target. It's fine.

Thus we can target bond, tie, link. That's 3.

Make sure "relationship" is not in opponent or distractor list: not.

Thus output:

CLUE: relationship
TARGETS: bond, tie, link

That is short, meets format
